description_long: This object consists of a large, vertical cylindrical tank made of brick-like material connected to a horizontal, capsule-shaped metallic chamber. The cylindrical tank stands upright and is connected to the chamber by a series of pipes, likely for fluid transfer. The horizontal chamber features several protruding elements, such as pipe inlets/outlets and metal brackets, and is capped at both ends. The surface of the chamber is smooth and metallic, while the cylindrical tank has a rough, brick-textured exterior. The pipes and connectors are metallic and run between the two main components, suggesting a functional relationship for pumping or processing fluids. The overall structure is robust and industrial, suitable for installation in urban utility areas.
description: A large, industrial pumping station with a vertical brick-textured tank connected to a smooth, metallic horizontal chamber by pipes and brackets.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded end of the metallic chamber and the vertical cylindrical tank behind it, with visible pipe connections.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the length of the horizontal chamber, the connecting pipes, and the full height of the cylindrical tank.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the cylindrical tank's brick texture, with only the edges of the horizontal chamber visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other end of the metallic chamber and the connection to the cylindrical tank.
